claim: OAuth was designed for 3-actor flows (user, app, identity provider) but agents create 4+ leg identity chains; traditional OAuth breaks with agent delegation
actionable: Don't use standard OAuth for agentic systems; implement token exchange (RFC 8693) and dynamic client registration (RFC 7591) for proper delegation chains
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Matthew Xu: The 4-Legged Identity Challenge

claim: 'Identity loss mid-chain' occurs when downstream APIs only see MCP server identity, not user identity; proper delegation requires token exchange for each hop
actionable: Ensure every agent-MCP-API hop exchanges tokens scoped to that resource; audit authorization logs to catch identity loss
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Matthew Xu: The 4-Legged Identity Challenge

claim: Token exchange (RFC 8693) is the cleanest identity delegation pattern but support is uneven (Keycloak good, Entra partial, Okta partial)
actionable: Choose identity provider based on token exchange support; Keycloak is safest choice for agent architectures requiring delegation
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Matthew Xu: The 4-Legged Identity Challenge

claim: Four RFCs work together for agentic identity: 9728 (metadata discovery), 8414 (authorization server config), 7591 (dynamic client registration), 8693 (token exchange)
actionable: Implement all four RFC standards as a stack; don't try to use subset without full delegation support
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Matthew Xu: The 4-Legged Identity Challenge

claim: Agents don't fit existing IAM systems designed for humans and static services; agents require new governance layer accounting for autonomy, context, and intent
actionable: Build custom authorization layers for agent systems; don't force agents into human-centric IAM models; track agent identity separately from user identity
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Matthew Xu: The 4-Legged Identity Challenge

claim: Centralized identity broker (like Agentic Fabric) solves multi-hop delegation better than pushing tokens into agent code; abstracts complexity from agent developers
actionable: For production agent systems, use identity broker layer to manage token lifecycle; keep agent code focused on reasoning and tool use
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Matthew Xu: The 4-Legged Identity Challenge
